title: 20 is pretty much fantastic Super rambly and spoilery
display_title: 20 is pretty much fantastic Super rambly and spoilery
wikitext:
[]: 2.0 is pretty much fantastic (Super rambly and spoilery) Post by SenorSquiid » Sat Apr 03, 2010 1:20 am I know I'm a little late to the party, but... I had seen 2.0 a few time before, but finally got the fansubs tonight. Oh my. It's amazing. I thought it was when I couldn't understand it, but I sometimes enjoy watching movies with no subtitles because a good film ought to be able to convey itself visually, and I was satisfied that it did. I got 1.0 and watched it for the first time that I remember (I watched it once so drunk i don't remember after the end of the first fight). So I decided to have a nice Eva nerd out night, and watched them back to back. 1. I can't stand the new dub cast, but fortunately was spared tio and only checked it sporadically. I really dislike the new Shinji. Now with extra whine. i've always kind of defended Shinji's character as not being a whiny emo kid, but pretty much what a kid would end up like if his estranged father who is into some occult illuminati shady business called me up and made me pilot a cloned monster alien/supernatural beast and fight aliens that regulalry put me staring death directly in the face ever few weeks while causing massive property damage and breaking people's houses and putting their sisters in the hospital and making him dream about some awful spooky train all the damn time. Shinji has a pretty rough life. But anyhow, 1.0 was nice and all, and it was awesome to see the classic iconic scenes done up all fancy and purdy. And there's some weird metafictional echoes. I don't buy the sequel theory, but I do think in some way the original universe has contaminated the new one, and it might not be a major plot point, but just a sort of indication of ripples, or it'[s taking the neon GENESIS angle to the max and doing some story of civilization wiping itself out and rebreeding from a base pair and it's just something that happens every million years or so. I don't know. anno's a crazy guy and i think he likes drugs. It seens like the kind of unspoken pretentious thing he'd do to have metafictional ripples of no signifigancce that have a reason, but mainly exist to mess with the fans. He's such a dude. But I think the key to rebuild is when Shinji's tape player switches to 27 and Kaji decides he can use Shinji for free labor and inspire shippers because that's the kind of guy anno is, and I guess mexicans didn't survive the 2nd impact, or you can't get 'em in the Geofront, so Kaji has 14 year old boys weed his mellon patch while he gets drunk (off screen of course) and rambles on about gardening and his half baked philosophy. Kaji's such an awesome character. But then everything changes. And you know what I always hated? The filler episode where Toji was chosen. The boring one before the really great episode with Unit 03. I'm happy that's gone. But when Shinji's tape goes to 27 is when the plot makes really substantial deviations. Adding Mari into the mix and the quick awesome fight with Asuka weren't really major changes. I guess Mari is, but that's the point where the actual plot switches rails from old Eva. Additionally, i really like how they focus a lot on the actual world that it takes place in, and actually showing the city being used as a weapon and as a platform to augment the Evas, and a lot of little details they've done, and how the damage to the city actually accumulates. Eva always had a lot of interesting ideas about it's setting but rarely explored them before, and i'm really digging those details. I like seeing Rei develop a bit more personality. The way she wants to see Shinji and Gendo happy together is definately an aspect of being Yui, but there's a defiante sort of romantic/sexual tension between them that's really setting this up for a pretty big twist eventually, and Anno doesn't care if it bugs you, because he's drunk on his own madness, and beer probably. One reason i always loved Eva is because it's basically one man's vision and staggering ego and self absorption funneled into giant robots and hot girls and it's just abotu the weirdest pop culture article ever because it revels in it's own perversion and deviance from norms, and with Rebuild, especially in 2.0 sort of challenges the norms it itself established, which is awesome. I love the show, I adore EoE, but I always thought, especially the show, was a little lacking on a lot of things because of the conditions under which it was created, and I love with rebuild that it's not a remake or reboot, but basically disassembling the original, cleaning it up, adding some new touches, reassembling and reframing it. Anno is a lucky man to get to create his masterpiece twice, and so far I think Rebuild could surpass the original in entirety. I love love love the last fight. I love ZA BEASTO, as you all call it. Which is awesome. Raging hard-core big as your head awesome. And Rei. And new Zeruiel is a monster, and then all of a sudden some horrible twisted version of the iconic Galactic Rei Tits. Oh no! Episode 19 is pretty much the high point of the series in my opinion, and they captured that and cranked it up,. Shinji hates everything, but he's damn well going to save Rei because she's the only connection he has to anything now. So What does he do? In a way he's sort of grown up. I kind of see the Rebuild characters beign the or9ginal, but having matured a bit. Almost like good karma from their past lives accumulated when they were reincarnated as themselves again. i try to be crazy like an anno to try to understand his thought processes. When I was right out of high school I spent a few months smoking weed and taking shrooms and watching EoE every week. Life was filled with madness. i'm not proud of everything I've done. But Shinji through sheer force of will powers the occult hellbeast and is totally conscious and directing everything. To draw a Gainax paralell, it reminds of of the part in FLCL where Naota tells Mamimi never to call him Takkun and has his badass moment. Shinji's all like "My arm is gone? I'LL FORM A NEW ONE! OH SHIT SON! ROCKET PUNCH! YOU LIKE THAT? YOU WANT SOME? LASER EYES! OH KABOOM! THAT'S RIGHT and basically becaomes a god because he's mad as hell and not going to take it any more and if treating the laws of nature like a fleshlight is what he needs to do to save his clone mom girlfriend person then that's cool because he is Shinji IKARI and he's not going to run away this time. So yeah. 2.0 is amazing.

[]: Post by Eva 04 » Sat Apr 03, 2010 1:51 am This was nice and all but you do know that Shinji has the same voice actor in 1.0 as the series,right?Same with Misato.Had to point that out.

[]: Post by SenorSquiid » Sat Apr 03, 2010 1:52 am Eva 04 wrote: This was nice and all but you do know that Shinji has the same voice actor in 1.0 as the series,right?Same with Misato.Had to point that out.

[]: Post by toe mash » Sat Apr 03, 2010 3:38 am Eva 04 wrote: This was nice and all but you do know that Shinji has the same voice actor in 1.0 as the series,right?Same with Misato.Had to point that out.

[]: Post by backseatjesus » Sat Apr 03, 2010 4:14 am SenorSquiid wrote: Really? Because he sounds loads worse.

[]: Post by Hatsumi92 » Sat Apr 03, 2010 7:21 am I've always liked the dub and sub equally. But i'll have to say i love Megumi Ogata's scream. It's just so awesome. As for 2.0, it's the reason I'm finding Rebuild much more enjoyable than Neon Genesis. It starts of great and just gets better. Watching 1.0 i was like "Yeah this is pretty good for a remake" and then when Ramiel shown up and did his stuff it kind of won me over. If Rebuild is better than Neon Genesis for one thing, it has to be the action.

[]: Re: 2.0 is pretty much fantastic (Super rambly and spoilery) Post by thewayneiac » Sat Apr 03, 2010 9:00 am SenorSquiid wrote: And you know what I always hated? The filler episode where Toji was chosen. The boring one before the really great episode with Unit 03. I'm happy that's gone.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 03, 2010 10:16 am No thoughts on Asuka? Did you like changes to her and/or felt she was rushed at all? Also, people have complained that 2.0 felt rushed in-universe due to 3rd Angel Angel battle, character drama, 7th Angel, drama, 8th Angel, drama, 9th Angel, drama, 10th Angel/character drama. What are your thoughts on such? Do you feel it worked?

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Apr 03, 2010 10:27 am But I think the key to rebuild is when Shinji's tape player switches to 27 and Kaji decides he can use Shinji for free labor and inspire shippers because that's the kind of guy anno is, and I guess mexicans didn't survive the 2nd impact, or you can't get 'em in the Geofront, so Kaji has 14 year old boys weed his mellon patch while he gets drunk (off screen of course) and rambles on about gardening and his half baked philosophy. Kaji's such an awesome character.

[]: Re: 2.0 is pretty much fantastic (Super rambly and spoilery) Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Apr 03, 2010 10:39 am SenorSquiid & almost everybody else wrote: Shinji WAS IN COMPLETE CONTROL DURING THE CLIMAX OMG!!1!!11

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 03, 2010 10:51 am I also thought Yui's control was obvious considering Shinji says something, Yui roars or reacts accordingly (Like Kyoko/Eva02 to Asuka in EoE). Besides, it's kinda impossible for Shinji to be piloting in the scene where his soul/body (skin melting off and all) reached Rei's soul/body inside the 10th Angel's core. >_>

[]: Post by Hatsumi92 » Sat Apr 03, 2010 11:36 am Just to clarify. When i say Shinji was awesome in the ending to 2.0, I'm kind of referring to the bit where he's screaming trying to get Rei back.

[]: Post by SenorSquiid » Sat Apr 03, 2010 1:43 pm To clarify, I means Shinji's English voice. Ogata does a fine job. The English delivery sends him just a little too far over the top into whiney land. I was talking about the dub on that point. While I'm at it, I dislike Gendo's new voice in the English version even more than Shinji's, because Gendo is where it's at. I suppose it would have been nice to have more Askuka time, but they made good use of the time she was in. I thought her entrance was perfect, and we'll see more of her in the next 2 films.I was pretty blown away at the end of 2.0 though thinking, "And there's still 2 more movies!" which is plenty of time to screw it up, but time to be awesome too. I'm sure Yui had something to do with Shinji activating Unit 01 god mode, but Shinji himself did too, and was definately conscious and in control as well. I'm nto trolling about Kaji, but rather being a bit flippant, and possibly reading into his motives. I mean, he seems a bit fatalistic some times and [rpbably reckoned he better find someone to take care of his garden if he gets assassinated again. Kaji is the kind of guy who thinks ahead. I really enjoyed the fast pacing. It moves everything along, and there wasn't any lack of Character development really, unless you're watching no subs. Maybe it does go a bit too fast for some, but I always though the show dragged in a lot of places where they didn't have quite enough story (or money) to flesh things out. I love the show, but it's very flawed and the pacing is uneven in a lot of parts. Episode 17 is filler. There's no real tension, everything points to Toji being selected and it ends up coming as no surprise when he is selected, and the character development was pretty shallow.The things in it were necessary for the show, but it didn't need it's own entire episode and could have been handled a lot better in 5 or 10 minutes.

[]: Post by Legendary » Sat Apr 03, 2010 2:03 pm There is no filler in NGE. Every episode builds to something. Episode 17's focus isn't actually Toji becoming pilot. It's about how messing around with Eva isn't a good thing, Kaji beginning to let Misato know Nerv's secrets (Code 707), and Shinji's slowly increasing mental instability.

[]: Post by SenorSquiid » Sat Apr 03, 2010 3:04 pm Legendary wrote: There is no filler in NGE. Every episode builds to something. Episode 17's focus isn't actually Toji becoming pilot. It's about how messing around with Eva isn't a good thing, Kaji beginning to let Misato know Nerv's secrets (Code 707), and Shinji's slowly increasing mental instability.

[]: Post by skikes » Sat Apr 03, 2010 3:12 pm When i seen the cam rip i was pretty disappointed but i was lucky enough to see it in the cinema in dublin a few weeks ago and the animation blew me away and i'm learning to accept the storyline for what it is. Something new and a lot more fun than what it was. Not that the original needed to be fun but its nice to have something different.

[]: Post by Eva 04 » Sat Apr 03, 2010 3:25 pm SenorSquiid wrote: To clarify, I means Shinji's English voice. Ogata does a fine job. The English delivery sends him just a little too far over the top into whiney land. I was talking about the dub on that point. While I'm at it, I dislike Gendo's new voice in the English version even more than Shinji's, because Gendo is where it's at.

[]: Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Apr 03, 2010 3:33 pm Eva 04 wrote: Shinji is voiced by Spike Spencer.Where the hell did you get this Ogata person from?

[]: Post by Legendary » Sat Apr 03, 2010 3:47 pm SenorSquiid wrote: Like most of episode 17.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Apr 03, 2010 4:05 pm Eva 04 wrote: Where the hell did you get this Ogata person from?
